channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Netflix reportedly considers adding always-on channels
item.description: Netflix is thinking about adding always-on channels that would stream specific shows and movies, according to The Wall Street Journal. The move sounds like a Netflix version of always-on services like Pluto TV and Tubi, except the big hook for those is that they're free - because of the ads you have to watch. Netflix […]

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Friday, July 10 (game #859)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Friday, July 10 (game #1628)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Friday, July 10 (game #1125)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: Days after announcing mass layoffs, Xbox CEO Asha Sharma tapped to advise the Federal Reserve on jobs
75 B-52 strategic bombers set to get gigabit connectivity and a fiber-optic network for their weapons pylons as part of a huge upgrade to keep them going till it reaches almost 100
item.description: American Air Force plans advanced digital weapons pylons with gigabit connectivity, supporting heavier munitions while extending B-52 service toward 100 years.

item.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
item.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promise improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

item.title: An AI agent startup just let its agent run its $100 million fundraise
item.description: Lyzr, a startup that builds AI agents for enterprises, used its own AI agent to raise a $100 million round — proof, evidently, that the product actually works.

item.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
item.description: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

item.title: The Soundcore AeroClip earbuds are $20 off and come with a free Soundcore Select 4 Go speaker
item.description: The Soundcore AeroClip earbuds are on sale for $149.99, down from the normal price of $169.99, and they come with either a free accessory or a free Soundcore Select 4 Go speaker

item.title: Quote of the day by NSA Whistleblower Edward Snowden on the 'nothing to hide' argument: 'No different than saying you don't care about free speech because you have nothing to say'
item.description: The whistleblower has exposed some of the most egregious surveillance programs in operation

item.title: Before you buy a new laptop, try this $10 Windows 11 Pro upgrade
item.description: Give a PC a major upgrade with a Microsoft Windows 11 Pro license.

item.title: Elon Musk praises Mythos/Fable, promises not to ‘cut off’ Anthropic
item.description: Should Anthropic trust Elon Musk to host its models? With about $40 billion in revenue at stake, Musk insists that the company can.

item.title: Playdate is helping Big Ben ring on time
Maine’s Senate Race Implodes, Meta’s Threads Rivals Musk’s X, and the Trump Phone Arrives
item.description: Today on Uncanny Valley, we unpack the political debacle unfolding in Maine surrounding the campaign of Democratic candidate Graham Platner.

item.title: Can AI answer the $3 trillion question?
item.description: The AI ROI debate has returned and the numbers are even bigger, as are, perhaps, the consequences.

item.title: Come to Our WIRED@NIGHT02 Film Screening!
item.description: See the documentary The Oldest Person in the World before it’s in theaters.

item.title: Singles in World Cup host continue to dust off their dating apps amid an influx of tourists
item.description: Soccer has become a staple for connecting online, while in-person dates and hookups with World Cup visitors are increasingly common. The 2026 World Cup has had no shortage of dramatic moments on the pitch. But action off the field for visitors and Americans is also heating up as the final matches draw closer.

item.title: Retroid Pocket 5 and Flip 2 are getting a spec bump, but will cost $10 more after July 14
item.description: Even older emulation-focused handhelds can't escape RAMaggedon.
